briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 17, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Demand for Declaration of Climate Crisis as a Health Emergency and Deepening Impacts
sections.content: Today, domestic and international experts strongly urged the World Health Organization (WHO) to declare the climate crisis an international public health emergency [1, 2]. The WHO also emphasized that climate change is a health crisis, discussing health, climate change, air quality, and energy issues at the 79th World Health Assembly [28, 32]. These warnings are leading to real crises, such as scientists' research findings that climate change is reducing oxygen levels in rivers worldwide [29, 39], and the record-breaking heatwaves experienced in Pakistan and India [41]. Concerns are growing that our vulnerable world is suffering from successive crises and that the situation will further deteriorate [34].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Current Status and Challenges of Domestic Carbon Neutrality Policy Implementation
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts are underway to achieve carbon neutrality goals; however, the need for policy implementation improvement has also been raised, with some lower-tier records reported in the Ministry of Economy and Finance's self-assessment of carbon neutrality policies [5]. Nevertheless, several local governments, including Gwacheon City, are implementing concrete carbon neutrality pledges and activities such as creating green parks, operating carbon neutrality points, and participating in environmental cleanup campaigns [6, 8, 9, 11, 15]. Furthermore, the selection of Gongju National University for a national project on carbon neutrality core technology development [13] and BNK Financial Group's achievement of SBTi global certification for greenhouse gas reduction targets [18] demonstrate active contributions from both corporate and academic sectors. These efforts are being made within a comprehensive response system, alongside the Presidential Committee on Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth [10].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Importance of Infrastructure Establishment
sections.content: Domestically and internationally, the acceleration of renewable energy transition is prominent, and by 2026, renewable energy is projected to surpass coal as the world's largest power source [24]. Gyeonggi Province has launched a working group to secure 10GW of renewable energy by 2030 [22], and SK E&S is also aiming to expand renewable energy supply [20]. Alongside this expansion, KEPCO Research Institute is striving to overcome the intermittency of renewable energy through ADMS and AI-based R&D [19], and 'microscopic climate information' is transforming the energy map [3]. However, a severe shortage of renewable energy facility operation and maintenance (O&M) personnel has been identified as a critical crisis domestically, highlighting the importance of infrastructure establishment for a stable energy transition [23].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Perception of Climate Change and Multifaceted Discussions on Response
sections.content: In the international community, diverse perceptions of climate change are being expressed, with active discussions, including global leaders holding a climate change summit in New York [33]. Meanwhile, political differences in approaches to climate change are also clearly evident, with Trump celebrating the UN Climate Committee's departure from the most extreme global warming scenarios [25]. There is also significant interest in the vulnerability of specific regions and their response strategies, such as research on the impact of climate change on Samoan children [38] or the idea that trees in Hong Kong could act as barometers amidst climate change [27]. The signing of an MOU between the Incheon Institute and the Korea Climate Change Research Institute to strengthen climate change response and international cooperation [4] signifies the importance of domestic and international collaboration, and discussions continue on multifaceted issues caused by climate change, such as water security [36] and animal welfare [26].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's daily briefing based on the climate-related news collected. It was a day that called for continued attention and effort in responding to the climate crisis.

articles.title: ‘It’s no longer exceptional’: Karachi struggles under brutal new reality of extreme heat
articles.description: Experts say the unseasonably hot weather across south Asia shows the impact of the climate crisisAn intense and prolonged heatwave has been causing misery for millions across Pakistan and India.In southern Pakistan throughout April and May, temperatures have risen far above seasonal norms. In Sindh, daytime temperatures have frequently crossed 44C to 46C, forcing residents indoors during peak afternoon hours and severely affecting outdoor labourers, transport workers and farming communities. Con

summary.phenomenon: An intense and prolonged heatwave has been observed across Pakistan and India, including southern Pakistan (Sindh region), throughout April and May. Daytime temperatures in the Sindh region frequently exceeded 44C to 46C, significantly surpassing seasonal averages.
summary.cause: Experts note that the unseasonably high temperatures across South Asia demonstrate the impact of the climate crisis.
summary.outlook: This heatwave is forcing residents indoors during peak afternoon hours and severely affecting outdoor laborers, transport workers, and farming communities. This is no longer an exceptional phenomenon but is establishing itself as a new reality.

articles.original_description: Experts say the unseasonably hot weather across south Asia shows the impact of the climate crisisAn intense and prolonged heatwave has been causing misery for millions across Pakistan and India.In southern Pakistan throughout April and May, temperatures have risen far above seasonal norms.
